user_query:
I’m a social studies teacher coordinating our PLC. Please build a small local event‑triggered automation that watches the workspace folder "input/" for new meeting agendas named "agenda_YYYY-MM-DD.md" and turns each into standardized notes and an action‑items CSV.

Requirements:
1) Implement a command‑line script at scripts/agenda_watcher.py with two modes:
   - --watch: monitor input/ for new files matching agenda_YYYY-MM-DD.md; when a new agenda appears, parse it along with input/roster.csv, produce outputs, and append a processed record (filename and timestamp) to out/trigger_log.json so the same agenda isn’t processed twice.
   - --once: run a single scan and process any unlogged agenda_*.md currently in input/.

2) Parsing and synthesis rules:
   - Read input/roster.csv to get the full participant list (name, role, email).
   - From each agenda_YYYY-MM-DD.md, extract these fields:
     • Date (YYYY-MM-DD)
     • Attendees (comma‑separated list of names on the Attendees line)
     • Decisions (bulleted list under the Decisions section)
     • Action Items (each line includes Task text with Owner, Due, and Priority in parentheses as shown in the sample agenda)
   - Compute Absentees by comparing the roster names with the extracted Attendees (case‑insensitive, trim whitespace). Any rostered name not listed as present is absent.

3) Outputs per agenda (use the date from the filename):
   - out/meeting_notes_YYYY-MM-DD.md containing:
     • Title: same as the first heading/title line in the agenda
     • Date: YYYY-MM-DD
     • Attendees (Present): list of names
     • Absentees: list of names computed from roster minus present
     • Decisions: list copied from the agenda
     • Action Items: a structured section that tabulates Task, Owner, Due, Priority, and defaults Status to "Open"
     • A short "Follow‑ups" bullet list that restates each action item in one sentence (e.g., "Jordan Kim to align unit pacing guide by 2024-09-22 (High)")
   - out/action_items_YYYY-MM-DD.csv with columns: task, owner, due_date, priority, status (status should default to Open for all rows).
   - Update/create out/trigger_log.json to record the processed agenda file name and a processed timestamp.

4) For this request, run the script in one‑shot mode on the provided input/agenda_2024-09-15.md and generate:
   - out/meeting_notes_2024-09-15.md
   - out/action_items_2024-09-15.csv
   - out/trigger_log.json reflecting that agenda_2024-09-15.md was processed.

No internet access is needed. Use only the provided local files under input/. Please leave the script in scripts/ and the outputs in out/ so I can verify them. Also include a brief note at the top of out/meeting_notes_2024-09-15.md listing any attendees you normalized (e.g., trimmed or case‑adjusted) so the matching with the roster is clear. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
